text:
§ 220.16 Criminal possession of a controlled substance in the third degree.

A person is guilty of criminal possession of a controlled substance in the third degree when he knowingly and unlawfully possesses:

1. A narcotic drug with intent to sell it; or

2. A stimulant, hallucinogen, hallucinogenic substance, or lysergic acid diethylamide, with intent to sell it and has previously been convicted of an offense defined in article two hundred twenty or the attempt or conspiracy to commit any such offense; or

3. A dangerous depressant or depressant with intent to sell it and he has previously been convicted of an offense defined in article two hundred twenty or the attempt or conspiracy to commit any such offense; or

4. A narcotic drug and said narcotic drug weighs one-half ounce or more; or

5. Cocaine and said cocaine weighs five hundred milligrams or more; or

6. A dangerous depressant and said dangerous depressant weighs ten ounces or more; or

7. A hallucinogen and said hallucinogen weighs twenty-five milligrams or more; or

8. A hallucinogenic substance and said hallucinogenic substance weighs one gram or more; or

9. A stimulant and said stimulant weighs one gram or more.

For purposes of this section, "controlled substance" shall have the meaning set forth in Public Health Law § 3302, and the schedules of controlled substances are set forth in PHL § 3306. Reference is also made to Penal Law § 220.00 for definitions applicable to this article and Penal Law § 220.18 for criminal possession in the second degree.

Criminal possession of a controlled substance in the third degree is a class B felony.
